observations.recommendation:
WerecommendedthattheManagement:
a) Direct theGSO and Inventory Committee toconduct a completephysical count
of all PPE, includingimmovable properties, and submitthe completedRPCPPE
withallsupportingdocumentations;
b) Direct the proper department to strictly implement the guidelines outlined in
COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to complete the one-time
cleansing of PPE accounts intended to establish PPE balances that are
verifiableastoexistence, conditionandaccountability;
c) Improve internal controls over all PPE management aspects, including regular
conduct of physical inventory, proper authorization/documentation, clear
responsibilities,a trackingsystem, maintenanceof propertycards, stockcards,
and all other documentations required for proper PPE management, in
accordancewithapplicableguidelines;
d) Make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the identified errors, including
those related to account reclassification, and ensure accurate reporting in
boththePPE Scheduleandthefaceof FS.

Werecommendedthat:
a) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conduct regular
reconciliation of the cash accounts balances so that errors/discrepancies be
adjusted accordingly, and to exert maximum effort in tracing and verifying the
accounts;
b) The Municipal Treasurer send written notice to the payees informing them
about the stale check for cancellation or replacement in accordance with
applicablelaws,rulesandregulations;
c) The Municipal Accountant preparenecessaryadjustingentries topresentfairly
thecashaccountscontrolledbytheLGU;
d) The LGU improve its internal controls over cash management and that the
accountable officers present all accountable forms noted during cash
examination.

We recommended that the Management adjust the financial records as of December
31, 2024, to accurately reflect the outstanding mandatory payable accounts (Due to
GSIS, PhilHealth,Pag-IBIG,and BIR) andstrictlyadheretoCOA Circular No. 2015-009,
ensuringthat mandatory payable accounts are debited only upon actual remittance to
therespective agencies.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to
record all procurement charged against LDRRM Fund and the corresponding
issuanceof goodstotheappropriateasset andexpenseaccounts.

We recommended that the management direct the concerned officials to
immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances. Send demand letters as
necessary. Moreover, cause the withholding of salary if no settlement received within
theprescribedperiod.

We recommended and the Management agreed to fast track the disposal of
unserviceableitems.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly ensure that the taxes
withheld from employees’ compensation and from payments to suppliers and
contractorsforgoodsdelivered andservices renderedareremitted intact andontime.

We, however, recommended and the Management agreed to review its internal
procedures to ensure proper oversight of payroll and remittance processes and
continue complying with RA No. 8291, or the GSIS Act of 1997, on the required
deductions of premiums and other related contributions/loans and timely remittance
thereoftotheGSISfor thebenefit of allemployees.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly enforce remittance of
premiums withheld to HDMF within the prescribed period to avoid incurrence of
penalties and interest, as well as prevent exposing the employees/beneficiaries to the
risksthat theycould not enjoythebenefits ofthe program.

We also recommended that Management continue to withhold and remit HDMF
contributions as mandated by RA No. 9679 to enable the Agency’s employees to
enjoyitsbenefitsand/or privileges.

Werecommendedthefollowingcoursesof actiontotheManagement:
a. Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO), the
Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator (MPDC) to set a specific timeline for the implementation of each
program,project or activityof theLDRRMF;
b. The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM Officer, prepare a complete and
comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include PPAs to be funded from the
currentappropriationsandtheunexpended previousyear’s balances;and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly submit the monthly Reports on
Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance
with Items 5.1.5 and 5.1.6of COACircular No. 2012-002.

Wethereforerecommendedto themanagement thefollowing:
a) To improve the completion rate of projects funded by the 20% DF, it is salient to
implement a more robust project monitoring and evaluation system. Regular
progress assessments, clear milestones, and deadlines should be established for
each program toensuretimelyexecution;
b) To address the discrepancy between the Financial Report and the Reports on
Checks Issued (RCI), a comprehensive reconciliation process should be put in
place. This process should involve cross-checking all financial documents and
transactions on a regular basis to ensure consistency and accuracy. Enhanced
internal controls, along with a review mechanism, should be introduced to detect
discrepancies early, and any inconsistencies must be corrected promptly to
ensuretheintegrityof financial reports; and
c) To comply with COA Circular 2004-006 and COA Circular 2012-001, the LGU
through the accounting division should enforce strict adherence to proper
documentation procedures for all disbursements and to ensure that a review and
confirmation of the legitimacy of payment evidence before approving any
disbursementsisconductedperiodically.

We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to submit
the financial reports, DVs with supporting documents, and the bank reconciliation
statements to the Commission within the deadline set in accordance with the
applicablelaws,rulesandregulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Direct the General Services Officer with the Inventory Committee to conduct complete
physical count and classify properly the accounts presented in the RPCPPE;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to
reconcile the balances per financial statements against the RPCPPE prepared by the
General Services Office and make full disclosures of the related information in the Notes
to the Financial Statements; and
c) Direct the concerned department to complete the one-time cleansing of PPE
accounts intended to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition
and accountability.

Management Comment:
During the exit conference, the Management expressed their intention to avail the one-time
cleansing of PPE to establish PPE balances. Additionally, the Management will dispose the
remaining unserviceable properties in the succeeding year.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Implement the increase in capitalization threshold from ₱15,000.00 to ₱50,000.00 and
direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) to undertake
necessary procedures provided under COA Circular 2022-004;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to reclassify the
properties from PPE accounts to Semi-Expendable Property accounts; and
c) Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to install measures/controls over semi-
expendable properties such as, but not limited to, update existing PPE and inventory
records, and prepare and maintain necessary forms/records/reports for purpose of record
keeping of these assets.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the
previously made entries by including the derecognition of the corresponding asset;
b) Enjoin the concerned department heads to comply with the applicable laws, rules and
regulations on the disposal of unserviceable properties; and
c) Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner and sound internal
control to achieve the fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular
reconciliation of the cash accounts balances so that errors/discrepancies be adjusted
accordingly, and to exert maximum effort in tracing and verifying the accounts;
b) Send written notice to the payees informing them about the stale check for cancellation or
replacement in accordance with applicable laws, rules and regulations; and
c) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to revert back to
cash accounts those stale and cancelled checks as of end of the year.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a) Direct the concerned officials to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances. Non-
compliance will constitute the valid cause for the withholding of the salary. Moreover, send
demand letters requiring the immediate liquidation of the cash advances to the concerned
employees; and
b) Strictly comply with the applicable rules and regulations pertaining to not granting any
additional cash advances unless the previous ones were settled first.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO), Municipal
Budget Officer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to set a
specific timeline for the implementation of each program, project or activity of the
LDRRMF; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance with Items 5.1.5 and
5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Management Comment:
The management agreed with our recommendations and assured the team to regularly submit
the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on time.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial reports, DVs and its supporting
documents and the bank reconciliation statements to the Commission within the deadline
set in accordance with the applicable laws, rules and regulations;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare detailed action plan to ensure the timely
submission of the transaction documents to the Office of the Auditor; and
c. Issue the necessary directive to inform and require all departments to adhere and commit
to the action plan and proposed improvements.

Management Comment:
The management agreed with our recommendations and committed to submit all the lacking
vouchers to the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the General Services Officer with the Inventory Committee to conduct complete physical count and classify properly the accounts presented in the RPCPPE;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to reconcile the balances per financial statements against the RPCPPE prepared by the General Services Office and make full disclosures of the related information in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
c. Direct the concerned department to complete the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts intended to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had initiated to conduct the one time cleansing of the properties.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office and GSO failed to reconcile their records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Implement the increase in capitalization threshold from ₱15,000.00 to ₱50,000.00 and direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer (GSO) to undertake necessary procedures provided under COA Circular 2022-004;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments to reclassify the properties from PPE accounts to Semi-Expendable Property accounts; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the GSO to install measures/controls over semi-expendable properties such as, but not limited to, update existing PPE and inventory records, and prepare and maintain necessary forms/records/reports for purpose of record keeping of these assets.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting journal entries to correct the previously made entries by including the derecognition of the corresponding asset;
b. Enjoin the concerned department heads to comply with the applicable laws, rules and regulations on the disposal of unserviceable properties; and
c. Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner and sound internal control to achieve the fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will ensure that the disposal of PPEs in the future will be in compliance with the applicable laws, rules and regulations on the disposal and accounting procedures were done in a timely manner.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular reconciliation of the cash accounts balances so that errors/discrepancies be adjusted accordingly, and to exert maximum effort in tracing and verifying the accounts;
b. Send written notice to the payees informing them about the stale check for cancellation or replacement in accordance with applicable laws, rules and regulations; and
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to revert back to cash accounts those stale and cancelled checks as of end of the year.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct the concerned officials to immediately settle the unliquidated cash advances. Non- compliance will constitute the valid cause for the withholding of the salary. Moreover, send demand letters requiring the immediate liquidation of the cash advances to the concerned employees; and
b. Strictly comply with the applicable rules and regulations pertaining to not granting any additional cash advances unless the previous ones were settled first.
observations.managementAction: The concerned officials were immediately notified and proactively followed up to liquidate their unliquidated advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO), Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to set a specific timeline for the implementation of each program, project or activity of the LDRRMF; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance with Items 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The management agreed with our recommendations and assured the team to regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial reports, DVs and its supporting documents and the bank reconciliation statements to the Commission within the deadline set in accordance with the applicable laws, rules and regulations;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare detailed action plan to ensure the timely submission of the transaction documents to the Office of the Auditor; and
c. Issue the necessary directive to inform and require all departments to adhere and commit to the action plan and proposed improvements.
observations.managementAction: The management agreed with our recommendations and committed to submit all the lacking vouchers to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
To the Municipal General Service Officer:
a. Conduct complete physical count and classify properly the accounts presented in the RPCPPE;
b. Complete the disposal procedures of the remaining unserviceable properties in coordination with the accounting department to derecognize the same in the books of the agency;
c. Follow the procedures set forth under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts of the LGU to establish the accountability, condition, and existence of PPEs; and
d. Institute measures in the monitoring and controlling of the properties of the LGU.
Municipal Accountant:
a. Prepare Journal Entries to derecognize the properties disposed; and
b. Ensure that the accounting procedures are done in a timely manner through sound internal controls in order to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had initiated to conduct the one time cleansing of the properties.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office and GSO failed to reconcile their records.

observations.managementAction: To the Municipal Accountant: Regularly submit the monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Fund to COA and other offices, in accordance with Sections 5.1.5 and 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation:
a. Address the causes of delay and/or non-implementation of various projects funded under 20%.
b. Revisit the AIP for the year as well as the continuing appropriations for projects not implemented for prior years and reevaluate the relevance of said projects to the present priority needs of the LGU; and
c. Evaluate the technical viability of proposed development projects and monitor the proper and timely implementation of 20% DF programs to attain optimal socio-economic and environmental management outcomes.

observations.managementAction: The LGU will conduct complete physical count in line with the onetime cleansing following the procedures set forth under COA Circular 2020-006. One time cleansing is currently on pre-planning stage.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management initiate the return of the unutilized balances to the respective Source Agency in accordance with Section 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and provisions of Memorandum of Agreement between the Implementing Agency and the Source Agency.

We recommended and Management agreed on the following:
a) The Municipal Accountant:
i. Issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation.
observations.managementAction: To notify and proactively follow up the accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads, address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of infrastructure projects in accordance with the approved plan and timelines to attain the desired economic growth as objective of the project; Revisit the AIP for the year as well as the continuing appropriations for projects not implemented for prior years and reevaluate the relevance of said projects to the present priority needs of the LGU; and Evaluate the technical viability of proposed development projects and monitor the proper and timely implementation of 20% DF programs to attain optimal socio-economic and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that management create an inventory committee to conduct the complete physical count of all PPE. A Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) should be accomplished, duly reconciled with the accounting records and submitted to the COA not later than January 31 of the ensuing year. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.
observations.managementAction: From then on, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO would maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.
observations.reason: Lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The LGU will evaluate the circumstances surrounding the procurement of this land and also follow-up on the processing of the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality. The books will be updated accordingly whatever the result of the evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to advise the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow Section 70 of the New Government Accounting System for the submission of the monthly, quarterly and semestral financial reports and Section 72 and 73 of the same manual for the submission of year-end reports. This will ensure timeliness of reports by the LGU as well as the auditor.
observations.managementAction: The municipal accountant committed to submit the monthly, quarterly and semestral financial reports and the year-end reports and personnel in accounting section will be augmented to handle the said task and facilitate immediate submission of said accounts and documents.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the management direct the ensuing
personnel the enumerated course of actions:
To the Municipal Accountant:
a. Maintain a manual PPE Ledger Cards to record the custody, estimated useful life,
depreciation, impairment and disposal of those assets;
b. Ensure that the accounting procedures are done in a timely manner, through proper
time management and sound internal control, in order to achieve a fair presentation
of financial statements.
To the Municipal General Service Officer:
a. Conduct complete physical count of all LGU properties together with other
members of the Inventory Committee of the LGU and report the same using
the prescribed RPCPPE form in NGAS Manual for LGUs;
b. Maintain proper and complete records of the PPEs, particularly the stock
cards and property cards as stipulated under the NGAS Manual for LGUs,
Volume I;
c. Conduct proper procedures to dispose of the unserviceable properties, in
coordination with the accounting department to derecognize the same in the
books of the agency;
d. Ensure that any information included in the report presents fair and accurate data
that is reliable and verifiable against supporting documents;
e. Institute measures in the monitoring and controlling of the properties of the
LGU.

Recommendations:
In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following:
To the Municipal Treasurer:
1) Post or record daily transactions in the cashbooks in chronological order and balance
and reconciled the same with the actual cash on hand;
2) Cashbooks shall be ruled, footed and closed monthly and shall be certified and the
same should be reconciled with the subsidiary/general ledger.
To the Municipal Accountant:
1) Submit all RCDs together with pertinent supporting documents to the office for
further verification.
2) Record collections and deposits in the books and reconcile the same with the
cashbook balance of the Treasury.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management, with the Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads:
1) Address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority
development programs and projects under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help
in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development of the LGU
and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment
Program.
2) Revisit the AIP for the year as well as the continuing appropriations for projects of
prior years and reevaluate the relevance of said projects to the present priority needs
of the municipality.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Management initiate the return of the unutilized balances
to the respective Source Agency in accordance with Section 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular 94-
013 dated December 13, 1994 and provisions of Memorandum of Agreement between the
Implementing Agency and the Source Agency.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Prepare detailed action plan to ensure the timely submission of the vouchers and
other financial reports to the Office of the Auditor, and for the Local Chief
Executive to issue the necessary directive to inform and require all departments
to adhere and commit to the action plan and proposed improvements;
b. Submit the remaining disbursement vouchers together with its supporting
documents and the RCDs with the duplicate copies of Official Receipts and
Validated Deposit Slips.; and
c. Strategize to get a copy of the bank statement as of each prescribed period by
requesting from its depository banks. Moreover, ensure that bank reconciliation is
done and submitted within the prescribed period as stated in the above-cited
regulations.

We recommended and the agreed that the Municipal Accountant: a) Prepare accounting journal entries to recognize the reversed /adjusted assets to ensure that the properties are duly accounted in the books and to effect the adjusting entry for the accounting of unrecorded expenses to present fairly the accounts in the financial statements; and, b) Ensure the proper recording of succeeding transactions affecting the bayanihan grant using the appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2020 Part II No. 1 An Accounting journal entry will be made to reverse/adjust assets to ensure the proper accounting of properties in the book.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Local Finance Committee (LFC), in coordination with the other local officials concerned, to prepare a monthly utilization report in the format provided in LBC 125 Annex C and regularly post it in at least three conspicuous public spaces and LGU’s website to keep the community informed and updated on the use of public funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2020 Part II No. 2 Require the Local Finance Committee (LFC), in coordination with the other local officials concerned, to prepare a monthly utilization report in the format provided in LBC 125 Annex C and regularly post it in at least three conspicuous public spaces and LGU’s website to keep the community informed and updated on the use of public funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a) The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, prepare a complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the current appropriations and the unexpended previous years’ balances; b) The Municipal Accountant immediately transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balances of the current year’s QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund; c) The Municipal Accountant record all procurement and issuance of food and medical supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2019 Part II No. 3 - The Municipal Accountant immediately transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balances of the current year’s QRF and to the Special Trust Fund; - The Municipal Accountant record all procurement and issuance of food and medical supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a) The Municipal Treasurer update the accounts and prepare refund to source agency. Also, to close the bank account/s with dormant deposit, if necessary. b) The Municipal Accountant make adjustment to record in the book the unrecorded cash in bank of the LGU to reflect the correct balance of cash in bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 Part II No. 4 - The Municipal Treasurer update the accounts and prepare refund to source agency. Also, to close the bank account/s with dormant deposit, if necessary. - The Municipal Accountant make adjustment to record in the book the unrecorded cash in bank of the LGU to reflect the correct balance of cash in bank accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a) The Municipal Accountant: i. Issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation; ii. Make a reclassification entry to appropriately account the Confidential Fund in the financial statements; and b) The LGU officials and employees strictly adhere to the regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 Part II No. 5 - Issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation; - Make a reclassification entry to appropriately account the Confidential Fund in the financial statements; - The LGU officials and employees strictly adhere to the regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads, address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development programs and projects under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development of the LGU and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program. Revisit the AIP for the year as well as the continuing appropriations for projects of prior years and reevaluate the relevance of said projects to the present priority needs of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 Part II No. 6 - The Municipal Accountant immediately transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balances of the current year’s QRF and to the Special Trust Fund; (Note: management personnel previously expressed that projects were untimely implemented because the LGU is undermanned of technical support personnel that can handle detailed engineering and programming works.)
observations.reason: Proper planning adjustment and schedule is being set and personnel are understaffed. Some PPAs were reprogrammed into other priority programs and projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create an inventory committee to conduct the complete physical count of all PPE. A Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) should be accomplished, duly reconciled with the accounting records and submitted to the COA not later than January 31 of the ensuing year. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 Part II No. 7 - Conduct complete physical count and submit RPCPPE; maintain PPE ledger cards and property cards.
observations.reason: Non reconciliation with the Accounting Records due to lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties from against third party claims and in the compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 Part II No. 8 To inquire with the DENR for possible issuance of title in favor of the LGU. ISF (Integrated Social Forestry) issued by the DENR to individual farmer/ occupant to affirm their ownership for such particular land for 25 years and is renewable for another 25 years.
observations.reason: The parcel of lands purchased by the LGU has a tenurial instrument called ISF in favor of individual farmer/ occupant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain and submit monthly to the COA the RAAF and all obsolete, spoiled and cancelled accountable forms to determine the accuracy and completeness of the reported collections and to prevent possible misappropriation and misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 Part II No. 9 Daily posting of cashbooks must be done to reconcile with the actual cash on hand and RAAF must be regularly submitted to the COA.
observations.reason: RAAF were not regularly submitted to the COA due to numerous number of workloads the employees were handling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the accountable officers strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations on cash management. Transactions should be posted or recorded daily in the cashbooks in chronological order. Subsequently, the cashbooks should be balanced daily and reconciled with the subsidiary/general ledger. Cashbooks shall be ruled, footed and closed monthly and shall be certified by the Municipal Treasurer and the same should be reconciled with the subsidiary/general ledger.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2018 Part II No. 10 Update posting in the cash books on a monthly basis.
observations.reason: Cashbooks were not updated daily due to numerous number of workloads the employees were handling.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to advise the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow Section 70 of the New Government Accounting System for the submission of the monthly, quarterly and semestral financial reports and Section 72 and 73 of the same manual for the submission of year-end reports. This will ensure timeliness of reports by the LGU as well as the auditor.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2016 Issued Memorandum Order to the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports on time to concerned offices and/or agency DVs to the LGU as well as the auditor. (Management enumerated that this was due to lack of personnel to segregate and prepare documents for submissions to COA.)
observations.reason: Monthly Trial Balance was not submitted due to delays on recording of liquidation reports and submission of paid DVs; lack of personnel to segregate and prepare documents for submission.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant:
a) Prepare accounting journal entries to recognize the reversed/adjusted assets to
ensure that the properties are duly accounted in the books and to effect the prior
period adjusting entry for the unrecorded expenses to present fairly the accounts
in the financial statements; and,
b) Ensure the proper recording of succeeding transactions affecting the
bayanihan grant using the appropriate accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Local Finance
Committee (LFC), in coordination with the other local officials concerned, to
prepare a monthly utilization report in the format provided in LBC 125 Annex C
and regularly post it in at least three conspicuous public spaces and LGU’s website
to keep the community informed and updated on the use of public funds.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a) The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, prepare a complete and
comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the current
appropriations and the unexpended previous years’ balances;
b) The Municipal Accountant immediately transfer all the
unexpended/unobligated balances of the current year’s QRF and DRRMF-
MOOE to the Special Trust Fund; and
c) The Municipal Accountant record all procurement and issuance of food and
medical supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are
charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement
of balances.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a) The Municipal Treasurer update the accounts and prepare refund to source
agency. Also, to close the bank account/s with dormant deposit, if necessary.
b) The Municipal Accountant make adjustment to record in the book the
unrecorded cash in bank of the LGU to reflect the correct balance of cash in
bank accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a) The Municipal Accountant:
   i. Issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers.
   Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or
   refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of
   the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation;
  ii. Make a reclassification entry to appropriately account the Confidential
   Fund in the financial statements; and
b) The LGU officials and employees strictly adhere to the regulations on the
granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed
period.

We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a. The Municipal Accountant should: i. Issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers. Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the regulation; ii. Make a reclassification entry to appropriately account the Confidential Fund in the financial statements; and b. The Municipal Treasurer will ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before processing another cash advance of the accountable officer concerned; and c. The LGU officials and employees should strictly adhere to the regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Demand letter has been issued by the Accountants Office to the accountable officer to settle their unliquidated cash advances. Reclassification entry has been made.
observations.reason: unliquidated due to lacking/loss supporting documents to liquidate the said cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed on the following: a) The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, prepare a complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the current appropriations and the unexpended previous years’ balances; b) The Municipal Accountant should: i. Record all procurement and issuance of supplies to the appropriate asset and expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger cards to monitor the movement of balances; ii. Facilitate the immediate reconciliation of accounts and transfer all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund; and iii. Make a reclassification entry to reflect the accurate balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account in the books; and c) The Inventory Committee, through the Municipal General Services Officer, must conduct the physical count of inventories, maintain stock cards and submit the reconciled inventory report to the Resident Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Early planning and discussions to prepare a comprehensive DRRMFIP to properly include the prior next appropriations and unexpended balances. Proper planning adjustment and implementation was discussed.
observations.reason: Municipal Engineering Office understaffed and personnel are pre-occupied; some PPAs were reprogrammed; certain reconciliations and transfers pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads, address the causes of delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development programs and projects under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development of the LGU and directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.
observations.managementAction: Proper planning adjustment and schedule is being set to ensure the efficient implementation of all priority programs and projects.
observations.reason: Municipal Engineering Office was understaffed and personnel are pre-occupied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure that only PPAs chargeable to 20% DF which shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socioeconomic development and environmental management outcomes of the LGU and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure are funded; and that the Municipal Planning and Development Council apply existing guidelines and policies in the utilization and funding requirements of the 20% Development Fund in preparation of the coming year’s DF allocation.
observations.managementAction: The concerns will be discussed with the members of the MDC as to compliance and alignment of PPAs based on existing guidelines and policies in the utilization and funding requirements of the 20% Development Projects in preparation of the coming year’s DF allocation;

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create an inventory committee to conduct the complete physical count of all PPE. A Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) should be accomplished, duly reconciled with the accounting records and submitted to the COA not later than January 31 of the ensuing year. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.
observations.managementAction: Conduct complete physical count
observations.reason: Non reconciliation with the Accounting Records due to lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties from third party claims and in the compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: To inquire with the DENR for possible issuance of title in favor of LGU. ISF (Integrated Social Forestry) issued by the DENR to individual farmer/ occupant to affirm their ownership for such particular land for 25 years and is renewable for another 25 years.
observations.reason: The parcel of lands purchased by the LGU has a tenurial instrument called ISF (Integrated Social Forestry).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain and submit monthly to the COA the RAAF and all obsolete, spoiled and cancelled accountable forms to determine the accuracy and completeness of the reported collections and to prevent possible misappropriation and misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Issued Memorandum Order to the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports on time to the concerned offices.
observations.reason: Posting on cashbooks were on weekly basis and RAAF were not regularly submitted to the COA due to numerous number of workloads the employees were handling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the accountable officers strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations on cash management. Transactions should be posted or recorded daily in the cashbooks in chronological order. Subsequently, the cashbooks should be balanced daily and reconciled with the subsidiary/general ledger. Further, it shall be certified by the Municipal Treasurer and the same should be reconciled with the subsidiary/general ledger.
observations.managementAction: Update posting in the cash books on a monthly basis to reconcile with the actual cash on hand and daily posting.
observations.reason: Cashbooks were not updated daily due to numerous workloads the employees were handling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to create a Project Monitoring Team (PMT) to undertake regular monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely and proper implementation of all the prioritized programs and projects under the 20% EDF so that the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes could redound to its intended beneficiaries. Also, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer should closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to allow prompt corrective action.
observations.managementAction: The Project Monitoring Team (PMT) must undertake speedy monitoring of pre-implementation, and close coordination with Planning and Engineering office.
observations.reason: Municipal Engineering Office was understaffed and personnel are pre-occupied; some PPAs were reprogrammed into other priority projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management demand full liquidation of outstanding cash advances from concerned officers and employees and apply stern measures such as withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand and stop the granting of additional cash advance. Ensure also strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and management of liquidation of cash advances, particularly to the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid accumulation of unsettled cash advances. Consider seeking authority for write-off of accounts from COA if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Issued demand letter to the concerned officials and employees to settle their cash advances.
observations.reason: Cash advances remained unliquidated due to lacking/loss supporting documents to liquidate the said cash advance. Management committed to further exert effort to collect and possibly seek authority for write-off of accounts from COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to advise the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow Section 70 of the New Government Accounting System for the submission of the monthly, quarterly and semestral financial reports and Section 72 and 73 of the same manual for the submission of year-end reports. This will ensure timeliness of reports by the LGU as well as the auditor.
observations.managementAction: Issued Memorandum Order 2016 to the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports on time to concerned offices.
observations.reason: Monthly Trial Balance was not submitted due to delays on recording of liquidation reports and submission of paid DVs to the Accounting Office; lack of personnel to segregate and prepare documents for submissions to COA.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant should:
i. Issue the final notice of demand to the accountable officers.
Withholding of salary/ies of officials and employees who still fail or
refuse to settle and/or liquidate their cash advances after the issuance
of the final demand may be instituted in accordance with the
regulation;
ii. Make a reclassification entry to appropriately account the Confidential
Fund in the financial statements.
b. The Municipal Treasurer will ascertain the presence of the “accountant’s
certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated” before
processing another cash advance of the accountable officer concerned; and
c. The LGU officials and employees should strictly adhere to the regulations on
the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed
period.

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Management agreed on the following:
a. The LDRRM Council, through the LDRRM officer, must prepare a
complete and comprehensive LDRRMFIP which should include both the
current appropriations and the unexpended prior year balances;
b. The Municipal Accountant should:
i. Record all procurement and issuance of supplies to the appropriate asset and
expense accounts which are charged against LDRRM Fund. Maintain ledger
cards to monitor the movement of balances of goods and supplies procured;
ii. Facilitate the immediate reconciliation of accounts and transfer all
unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the
Special Trust Fund account;
iii. Make a reclassification entry to reflect the accurate balance of Trust
Liabilities-DRRMF account in the books; and
c. The Inventory Committee, through the Municipal General Services
Officer, must conduct the physical count of inventories. Likewise, the
Municipal General Services Officer shall maintain stock cards and
submit the reconciled inventory report to the resident auditor.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management, with the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer as leads, address the causes of
delays and ensure the efficient implementation of all priority development programs
and projects under the 20% LDF that are envisioned to help in the attainment of
social, economic and environmental development of the LGU and directly
supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Council
include only PPAs chargeable to 20% DF which shall contribute to the attainment
of desirable socioeconomic development and environmental management outcomes
of the LGU and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure.

We recommended that management create an inventory committee to conduct the complete physical count of all PPE. A Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) should be accomplished, duly reconciled with the accounting records and submitted to the COA not later than January 31 of the ensuing year. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of assets.

observations.reason: Non reconciliation with the Accounting Records due to lacking documents

observations.reason: The parcel of lands purchased by the LGU has a tenurial instrument called ISF (Integrated Social Forestry) issued by the DENR to individual farmer/ occupant to affirm their ownership for such particular land for 25 years and is renewable for another 25 years

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended that management create an inventory committee to conduct the complete
physical count of all PPE. A Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) should be accomplished, duly reconciled with the accounting records and submitted
to the COA not later than January 31 of the ensuing year.

Likewise, the Municipal Accountant and MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger
cards and property cards, respectively, for each property for easy reference and reconciliation of
assets.

Recommendation
We recommended that management make a follow-up on the processing of the titling of
the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality to safeguard the properties against third party
claims and in compliance with Section 449 of GAAM, Volume I.

Recommendation:
1. To ensure strict utilization of LDRRM funds for calamity/disaster-related
programs/activities in order to align with the funds’ purpose and objectives.
2. To revisit, review, and evaluate the programs/projects/activities prepared in the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) of the
Municipality to ensure effective formulation implementation and utilization of
LDRRM Fund.

Recommendation
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer maintain and submit monthly to the COA
the RAAF and all obsolete, spoiled and cancelled accountable forms to determine the accuracy
and completeness of the reported collections and to prevent possible misappropriation and misuse
of government funds.

Recommendation
We recommended that the accountable officers strictly comply with the existing rules and
regulations on cash management. Transactions should be posted or recorded daily in the cashbooks
in chronological order. Subsequently, the cashbooks should be balanced daily and reconciled with
the actual cash on hand. Further, it shall be ruled, footed and closed monthly and shall be certified
by the Municipal Treasurer and the same should be reconciled with the subsidiary/general ledger.

Recommendation
We recommended to the management to create a Project Monitoring Team (PMT) to
undertake regular monitoring of project implementation to ensure timely and proper
implementation of all the prioritized programs and projects under the 20% EDF so that the desired
socio-economic development and environmental outcomes could redound to its intended
beneficiaries at the soonest time possible.

Also, the Municipal Planning and Development Council Officer and Municipal Engineer
should closely monitor the implementation of the proposed programs and projects. The monitoring
procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress or delays in the implementation to
assist the management in the performance review and in identifying gaps in implementation to
allow prompt corrective action.

Recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we reiterated prior year’s recommendation for the
inventory committee to conduct complete physical count of all PPE and immediately
reconcile the report with the accounting records to ensure fair presentation the balances of
PPE accounts in the financial statements.

Recommendation
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare
necessary adjusting entries to record the appropriate liability for the funds received from LGUs,
NGAs and GOCCs in accordance with the Revised Chart of Account for Local Government Units
(RCALGU).

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management demand full liquidation of outstanding cash
advances from concerned officers and employees and apply stern measures such as
withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the officials/employees who
continuously disregard, fail or decline to comply with the demand and stop the granting of
additional cash advance.

Ensure also strict compliance with COA Circulars 97-002 on the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances, particularly the period within which to liquidate the same to avoid
accumulation of unsettled cash advances.

Recommendation
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall prepare
necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the subject cash advances to its appropriate account in
accordance with the Revised Chart of Account for Local Government Units (RCALGU).

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Management, through the Municipal Planning and Development
Council Officer and Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of the proposed
programs and projects. The monitoring procedure should include periodic reporting of the progress
or delays in the implementation to assist the management in the performance review and in
identifying gaps in implementation to allow prompt corrective action.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Management was also enjoined to reassess/reevaluate the Prior Years’ unimplemented
projects and determine whether the same remains as its priority projects or they need to be re-
programmed to other more significant ones.

We recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:
a. Observe strictly the requirement set forth in Section 7 (b) of the Republic Act No. 10410
by allocating in Local School Board Budget adequate funds for various activities in
support of the implementation of the ECCD Program, and comply with the guidelines
on allowable charges against the Special Education Fund set forth in Section 4 of Joint
Circular No. 1. s. 2017 of DepEd, DBM and DILG; and
b. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the local ECCD Council, Child
Development Teachers, Day Care Workers and other stakeholders of the ECCD System
in planning and budget preparation for the implementation of the ECCD program.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer Office to submit the RCI together with the paid Disbursement Vouchers and other supporting documents, Report of Collections and Deposits (RCDs) within the prescribed period under the NGAS.
observations.managementAction: However, management commented that personnel in accounting section will be augmented to handle the said task and facilitate immediate submission of said accounts and documents.
observations.reason: Accounts from 2016 were all concerned office submitted but the not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: Cause the strict compliance of the requirements on the frequency of deposits of collections that the same should be deposited intact daily or not later than the next banking day with the authorized depository bank. The Municipal Treasurer and the Special Disbursing Officer should update/post entries in the cashbooks and reconcile the book balance with the cash on hand daily. They shall foot and close the books at the end of each month to facilitate periodic reconciliation with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office to have a regular deposit of all collection at least once a week and to have an updated reconciled records of cash the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for management to fast track the completion of the project by following the guidelines on termination of contract for a cause. Under the guidelines, it may enter into a negotiated procurement pursuant to Section 53.3 of the Procurement Law and its Implementing Rules and Regulations – A. “The contract may be negotiated starting with the second lowest calculated/highest rated bidder for the project under consideration at the bidder’s original bid price. If negotiation fails, then negotiation shall be done with the third lowest calculated/highest rated bid at his original price. If negotiation fails again, a short list of at least three (3) eligible contractors shall be invited to submit their bids, and negotiation shall be made starting with the lowest calculated/highest rated bid.” If section 53.3 fails, then LGU may resort to implement the project by administration to prevent further loss and damage on the part of the government.
observations.managementAction: The Local Government returned the unexpended balance to the funding agency after the Barangay conducted general assembly and decided that they were no longer interested of the project.
observations.reason: This was due to the NPA burned the heavy equipments of the contractor due to non- settlement of the revolutionary tax.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances and the strict adherence to prescribed rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and liquidation of Cash Advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR Issued CY 2016 demand letter to the concerned officials and employees to settle their cash advances based on the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Cash advances from prior years were not settled and unliquidated due to lacking/loss supporting documents on the prescribed period. However, the management committed to further exert effort to collect and possibly seek authority for write-off of accounts from COA.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the Bank Statements, debit/credit memos, cancelled and paid checks and all other supporting documents necessary to facilitate its timely review and verification.
observations.managementAction: Monthly BRS were prepared but were submitted. However, BRS as December were attached on the Financial Statements.
observations.reason: BRS were prepared but were not submitted due to delays on recording of liquidation reports and submission of paid DVs to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for the inventory committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of all properties and reconcile immediately the results of the count with the property and accounting records to ensure correctness and accuracy of the recorded/reported PPE in the FS.
observations.managementAction: MGSO conducted the physical inventory for 2017 but reconciliation was not made with the records of the Accounting Office. Management committed to conduct reconciliation of PPE in ensuing year.
observations.reason: Inventory committee was not created to conduct the physical inventory. However, the MGSO conducted the physical inventory for 2017 but reconciliation was not made with the records of the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: Require the concerned Accountable Officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances and the strict adherence to prescribed controls in granting, utilization and liquidation of Cash Advance.
observations.managementAction: Issued Demand Letter to the Official/ Employee with unliquidated cash advance.
observations.reason: Some cash advances were not liquidated in accordance with the prescribed rules and regulations, we reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the follow ing:

The Inventory Team should prioritize the reconciliation of the result of the inventory count with the property and accounting records.  Likewise,the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO should maintain complete PPE ledger cards and property cards, respectively.

For tangible assets with estimated useful life of more than one year but small enough to be considered as property, plant and equipment, the Municipal Accountant should effect the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management has conducted physical inventory count of PPE but not reconciled with the accounting records
observations.reason: Property and accounting records not reconciled, we reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: Comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions and DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5-A dated October 7, 2004.
observations.managementAction: Management complied and submit the said documents

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management require the Municipal Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report to be approved by the head of the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board (GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.
observations.managementAction: Management complied and submit the said reports

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the remaining Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) amounting to P151,414.50 be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund of the National Government thru the account of the Treasury of the Philippines pursuant to the Supreme Court ruling.
observations.managementAction: Management returned the unutilized PDAF.

observations.recommendation: Discontinue the use of open dump sites and identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as final disposal site for solid wastes.  This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned under RA 9003. Implement strictly the proper segregation of solid wastes thru massive information drive/ campaign and awareness program for the residents.
observations.managementAction: Management is now using sanitary landfill
